P1.label: Market Monopoly
P1.description: Holds a disproportionate share of sector contracts — potential market capture through non-competitive means.

P2.label: Ghost Company
P2.description: Exhibits shell-company indicators: short operating history, few contracts, near-100% direct awards, unverifiable presence.

P3.label: Intermediary
P3.description: Acts as a pass-through entity, winning contracts outside its primary industry and likely reselling to actual providers.

P4.label: Bid Rigging
P4.description: Multiple vendors show coordinated bidding — rotation, cover bids, or geographic market allocation.

P5.label: Price Manipulation
P5.description: Contract values are statistically anomalous vs. sector benchmarks, indicating inflated pricing.

P6.label: Institutional Capture
P6.description: Receives a disproportionate share from a single institution, typically through direct awards.

P7.label: Network Cluster
P7.description: Embedded in a co-contracting network, suggesting coordinated vendor rings.

subject.introText: This analysis thread follows the procurement history of {{name}}, tracing the patterns, relationships, and financial flows that triggered automated detection. Scroll to explore the analysis.

timeline.heading: {{total}} Contracts, {{minYear}}–{{maxYear}}
timeline.dotDescription: Each dot is one year. Size reflects total value. Color reflects average risk. Hover for details.
timeline.hourglassDescription: A two-channel reading. Above the year axis: how many contracts each year produced. Below: how much money flowed. When one channel diverges from the other — a single contract worth billions, or a swarm of small contracts adding to nothing — the mismatch is the signal.
timeline.hourglassLegend: ▲ ABOVE = CONTRACT COUNT · ▼ BELOW = LOG(VALUE) · COLOR = AVG RISK
timeline.eraNarrative: opens in {{year}}. {{share}}% of total value (≈ {{value}}) flowed during high-risk years.

pattern.heading: What the Algorithm Found
pattern.description: Risk scores are built from 16 z-score features normalized against sector baselines. These are the factors that drove this vendor's score.
pattern.diagnosis: {{anomalous}} of {{total}} lab values outside sector reference range
pattern.noData: Feature attribution data not available for this vendor.
pattern.shapNote: Positive values increase risk score (red). Negative values reduce it (green). Values are SHAP-style feature contributions: β_i × (z_i − E[z_i]).

network.heading: Connections & Relationships
network.description: Vendor networks reveal co-contracting rings, shared entities, and institutional dependencies invisible in contract-by-contract analysis.
network.constellationDescription: A reach topology. The outer ring marks the institutions this vendor served; the inner ring marks the vendors who bid alongside them. The shape exposes whether this vendor operates inside a network or as a single source.

topology.tightSingleSource: Tight single-source profile. No co-bidders, narrow institutional reach — typical of capture or sole-source contracts.
topology.broadSingleSource: Broad single-source profile. No coordinated co-bidding, but contracts spread across many institutions — possible direct-award dominance.
topology.thinNetwork: Thin co-bidding network. The vendor occasionally bids alongside a small set of others.
topology.denseNetwork: Dense co-bidding network. The vendor consistently bids alongside the same group — review for rotation or decoy patterns.

network.institutionalRibbonHeading: Where the relationships landed, year by year
network.institutionalRibbonDescription: Each lane is one institution. The ribbon spans the years that institution received contracts from this vendor; thickness encodes total value, color encodes average risk. Sorted top-down by money received. Click any lane to open the institution.

institutionsNote.few: Very few institutions — possible institutional capture.
institutionsNote.many: Wide institutional reach — diversified vendor profile.

sectorsNote.multi: Cross-sector activity — may indicate intermediary behavior.

network.openNetworkGraphDesc: Interactive force-directed graph showing co-bidders, related entities, and community clusters.
network.coBidderAnalysisDesc: Vendors that consistently appear in the same procedures — potential coordination rings.

money.heading: {{value}} Over Time
money.description: Annual contract value and risk trajectory — where the money went, and when the risk spiked.
money.staircaseDescription: The journey of money. Each step is a year. Step color is the average risk that year. The taller the step, the larger the year-over-year jump — pinned annotations mark the three largest. The final number is the cumulative total.
money.riskNote: Risk thresholds: Critical ≥60%, High ≥40%, Medium ≥25%. OECD benchmark: 2–15% high-risk.

verdict.heading: Investigation Summary
verdict.description: Statistical signals warrant attention. A high score is not proof of wrongdoing — it means this vendor closely resembles documented corruption patterns.
verdict.questionText: Does this vendor's procurement record warrant investigation?

finding.low: No standout signals against sector baseline.

verdict.methodologyNote: Risk scores measure similarity to documented corruption patterns in 3.05M Mexican federal contracts (2002–2025). Scores are statistical indicators for investigation triage — not proof of wrongdoing. Model v0.6.5, vendor-stratified validation, AUC 0.798 (train) / 0.828 (test). See
